sentences.text: Sometimes, the neighbors thought it was strange that the rich Miller never gave little Hans anything, even though he had a lot of flour, cows, and sheep; but Hans didn't worry about this and loved listening to the Miller talk about true friendship.
Little Hans worked hard in his garden.

He was very happy during the spring, summer, and autumn, but when winter came, he had no fruit or flowers to sell, so he often felt cold and hungry and sometimes went to bed with only a few dried pears or nuts.
In winter, he was also very lonely because the Miller never visited him.
